title: Log architecture: split ComfyUI/subprocess output out of app.log, and reconcile line-trim vs byte-rotation
description:
EXPLORATORY — user wants options discussed, not a predetermined fix. Bring proposals; do not just implement the first idea below.

MEASURED POLLUTION (2026-07-19, both live log files):
- logs/app.log, 2605 lines: [comfy] 1795 (69%), [system] 701 (27%, mostly pip subprocess), [download] 71, [server] 6, [ui] 3, [runpod] 2, [main] 2. Actual app categories = 84 lines = 3.2%.
- logs/app.log.1, ~14970 lines: [comfy] 14779 (98.7%). App categories = 189 lines. Roughly 1 line in 79 was about the app.
Both retention policies are sane — they are just being consumed by subprocess output. app.log.1 covered only Jul 16 23:00 -> Jul 18 07:29 (33h) before hitting 2MB.

CONCRETE HARM SEEN THIS SESSION (MPI-310): while investigating the shared-dep deletion, app.log had gaps (13:00-15:00 missing) and could not confirm or deny whether an LTX uninstall ever ran, because history had been evicted. An 'absence of evidence' reading was nearly taken as proof of absence.

TWO MECHANISMS BOTH LIVE in routes/logger.js (user was unaware of the second; both are real):
1. MAX_LOG_LINES=2500 -> _trimLogFile() at STARTUP ONLY: reads the WHOLE file into memory, splits, keeps the last TRIM_TO_LINES, rewrites in place. DESTRUCTIVE — trimmed lines gone, no backup. Swallows all errors (catch(_){}) so a failed trim is silent and leaves the log unbounded. Runs before _ready.
2. MAX_LOG_BYTES=2MB -> rename to app.log.1 during writes. RECOVERABLE, one generation kept.
User's view: byte rotation is the better system. Open question: does the startup line-trim still earn its place at all, given rotation covers the same job non-destructively? It currently destroys history rotation would have preserved.

HARD CONSTRAINT — DO NOT BREAK: app.log is what users attach to GitHub issue reports (the reporter captures its last N lines). Whatever ships, the file at that path must stay the useful thing to paste into a bug report. Verify the reporter path FIRST: grep the GitHub-report / error-dialog code for how it reads the log and how many lines it takes.

USER'S STARTING IDEA (their words, explicitly 'throwing things out there'): decouple ComfyUI terminal output into its own log, so there is an app-only log to read when the issue is known not to be ComfyUI. Leave app.log alone for reporting.

OPTIONS TO WEIGH (not exhaustive — bring others):
(a) Separate sink per source: comfy.log for [comfy]/[system] subprocess output, app.log for the rest. Cleanest signal; risk = a ComfyUI crash that IS the app's problem now lives in the other file, so a GitHub report may lose the relevant context. Mitigate by having the reporter attach BOTH tails.
(b) Keep one app.log for reporting, add a filtered app-only file written in parallel (dual write). No reporting risk; costs a second file + write path.
(c) Keep one file, add per-category retention (comfy lines evicted first). Single source of truth preserved; more complex eviction.
(d) Reduce [comfy] volume at the source: most of it is ComfyUI stdout echoed verbatim (progress bars, node loads, pip output). Level-gate or sample before it reaches the sink. Cheapest if those lines are genuinely low-value — CHECK what the 1795 lines actually are before assuming.

Deliverable for the picking session: a recommendation with the reporting-path impact stated explicitly, then implement on user's go.

ALSO IN SCOPE — .gitignore misses the rotation target (verified 2026-07-19):
`.gitignore:50` has `*.log`, which covers logs/app.log and logs/_phaseA_launch.log — but NOT `logs/app.log.1`, because its extension is `.1`, not `.log`. So the byte-rotation feature creates a file its own ignore rule does not cover: app.log.1 (2MB, currently ~98.7% ComfyUI output) sits UNTRACKED in every dev's tree, one `git add -A` away from being committed. Repo convention forbids `git add -A` (CLAUDE.md, commit by explicit pathspec), which is why this has not bitten yet — but the exposure is real and silent.
FIX is one line, but pick the pattern deliberately alongside whatever this card decides about log layout, so it covers the FINAL set of files rather than being patched twice: `logs/*.log.*` covers numbered rotations; `logs/` covers the whole dir (simplest if no log file should ever be tracked — check _phaseA_launch.log is genuinely disposable first, it is from 2026-06-22 and may be a kept artifact). If this card introduces a second sink (comfy.log etc.), the ignore rule must cover its rotations too.

--- SHIPPED 2026-07-19 (commit 6403fe83) ---
DECISION (user): aggressive source-filter, NO second sink yet. User has terminal
access during debugging, so INFO detail dropped from the file is still readable
live via the console mirror in _write().

RETRACTED from the original proposal: expanding the reporter's 2000-char slice.
User corrected this — the slice was trimmed to that size EMPIRICALLY because
larger payloads failed to submit to GitHub. It stays at 2000. The filter helps
it anyway: those last 2000 chars now carry app events, not tqdm bars.
PARKED: R2-upload-full-log-and-link idea. User said leave it. Own card if wanted.

Done:
1. routes/comfy.js — COMFY_LOG_NOISE (13 patterns) + _isComfyLogNoise(), gating
   the FILE log only, for 'info'-classified lines only. Placed before the logger
   call; all downstream progress/SSE parsers still get raw text (MPI-147 safe).
   ANSI escapes stripped from kept lines.
2. routes/logger.js — _trimLogFile() DELETED with MAX_LOG_LINES/TRIM_TO_LINES.
   Header now warns against reintroducing it. Rotation is the only retention.
3. .gitignore — `logs/` added (`*.log` never matched app.log.1).

MEASURED (replaying both real logs through the shipped patterns):
- app.log   3373 -> 1348 lines (60.0% smaller)
- app.log.1 15125 -> 4673 lines (69.1% smaller)
- 0 of 844 diagnostic lines lost across both files.

KEY FINDING — the two files had DIFFERENT noise profiles, and testing only the
current app.log would have shipped a filter that barely touched the 15k file:
app.log is generation churn (tqdm), app.log.1 is BOOT churn (~136 engine boots
x ~30 extra_model_paths lines + 1005 node-import timings). Both are covered now.
The first pattern set cut app.log 61% but app.log.1 only 1.4%.

STILL OPEN:
- NOT verified against a live app run — the filter was validated by replay only.
  Needs one real session: boot the app, generate, confirm the status bar still
  ticks (MPI-147 progress parsers) and app.log stays lean.
- comfy.log split (option a): DEFERRED, likely unnecessary now. Revisit only if
  the filtered volume still drowns app.log after a real session.
- Other subprocess sinks still pipe raw stdout unfiltered: routes/shared.js
  (pip [system], custom-cmd), routes/engine.js + routes/gitProvision.js
  ('engine'). [system] was 27% of app.log. Not touched — only [comfy] was the
  measured problem. Filter them if they become the new top talker.

--- STOPPED 2026-07-19 (user call: diminishing returns) ---
SHIPPED + WORKING (the actual card goal):
- Destructive startup line-trim DELETED; rotation is the only retention.
- MAX_LOG_BYTES 2MB -> 256KB (~2000 lines/file, ~512KB both). app.log.1 can no
  longer reach 15k lines. Old app.log.1 + _phaseA_launch.log deleted.
- logs/ gitignored (was: `*.log` never matched app.log.1).
- logger.consoleOnly()/fileOnly(): terminal keeps FULL Comfy output, file gets
  the filtered subset. Console mirror verified intact both directions.
- CLAUDE.md: agents told not to read app.log.1 unprompted.
RESULT: log went from 15125 lines to ~237 after a clean restart. The
context-burn problem that opened this card is SOLVED.

KNOWN UNRESOLVED (deliberately abandoned — NOT worth more restarts):
~132 boot-banner lines ("Adding extra search path", "setup plugin") still reach
app.log per boot, despite _isComfyLogNoise() provably matching those exact
strings when the shipped code is eval'd against them in isolation (verified).
So the patterns are right and something between _filterComfyChunk and the file
write is not behaving as read. Suspected but UNPROVEN: a second writer, or the
per-line path not being the one that executes for those chunks. Ruled out:
stale module cache (persisted across a clean npm start), renderer echo (no
comfy:log path exists), pattern correctness (tested directly).
DO NOT reopen unless the log becomes a problem again. At 237 lines it is not.

ALSO STILL UNVERIFIED: MPI-147 status-bar progress under the new filter. The
parsers sit downstream of the log call and were not intentionally touched, but
no one has watched a generation tick since. Check opportunistically.

events.note: Raised by user during MPI-310 after discovering app.log.1 and learning both a 2500-line startup trim and a 2MB byte rotation are live simultaneously. Pollution measured at 69% [comfy] in the current log, 98.7% in the rotated one. User prefers byte rotation, wants ComfyUI output decoupled, and flagged that app.log must remain the GitHub-report artifact.

Scope added: .gitignore's `*.log` (line 50) does NOT match logs/app.log.1 — the rotation target is untracked and uncovered, 2MB sitting in every tree. Fix is one line but should be chosen alongside this card's log-layout decision so it covers the final file set (incl. any new sink's rotations) instead of being patched twice. NOTE the original report of this said 'logs/ is neither tracked nor gitignored' — that was WRONG; app.log and _phaseA_launch.log ARE ignored. Only the .1 rotation slips through.

SHIPPED 6403fe83 — comfy stdout noise filter + _trimLogFile() deleted + logs/ gitignored. Replay-verified: app.log -60%, app.log.1 -69%, 0/844 diagnostics lost. Reporter 2000-char slice left alone (user: bigger payloads fail to submit). NEEDS a live app run to confirm MPI-147 progress parsers still tick. comfy.log split deferred as likely unnecessary.

STOPPED by user — diminishing returns. Core goal achieved: 15125-line log -> ~237 lines; destructive trim gone, 256KB cap, logs/ ignored, terminal output preserved via consoleOnly. Abandoned: ~132 boot-banner lines still leak per boot despite patterns provably matching in isolation; cause not found, not worth further restarts at this log size. MPI-147 progress tick still unwatched.
